title: Kary Mullis
summary: American biochemist who invented the polymerase chain reaction (PCR) in 1983. PCR amplifies DNA exponentially via cycles of heat-driven denaturation, primer annealing, and polymerase extension. Mullis won the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1993 for the discovery. PCR is now indispensable in forensics, paternity testing, medical diagnostics, and basic research.
